occurrences.line_text: | done | Harden quality lifecycle caps and incident subjects | — | Watchlist lifecycle caps now apply to non-hypothesis rows with bad quality fields as well as hypothesis-derived rows: local-only/exploratory, zero-score, insufficient impact/evidence/source, or `candidate_role=unknown_with_reason` rows cannot safely remain active watchlist/high-priority unless they persist an explicit non-active final state and cap reason. Stale persisted final states are recomputed when quality fields exist, and missing-quality fresh alert/playbook rows receive conservative local-only defaults. Artifact doctor now reports universal, hypothesis, non-hypothesis, capped, uncapped, legacy, diagnostic, invalid, and garbage-subject incident counts. Incident subject validation rejects boilerplate/prose/SEO fragments such as `Actions`, `Announcements`, `However`, `It`, `LLM`, `Non`, `Note`, `Only`, `Best Prediction Market Apps`, and `Polymarket Invite Code SBWIRE`; `Polymarket World Cup Volume` normalizes to `World Cup` when sports context exists. Existing garbage-subject artifacts are quarantined at read/report time and hidden unless diagnostics are explicitly requested. |

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make canonical incidents the Event Alpha state spine | — | Incident identity now propagates as top-level `incident_*` aliases across impact hypotheses, hypothesis-store rows, hypothesis-derived watchlist rows, alert snapshots, run-ledger rows, incident reports, and artifact doctor checks. Hypothesis watchlist keys use `incident_id + asset/sector identity + candidate_role + impact_path_type` when available, so duplicate source updates enrich the same incident/watchlist row instead of fragmenting state. Material update reasons now include incident-specific independent-source, cause-status, claim, market-reaction, causal-mechanism, and role-change reasons. Strict artifact doctor checks block fresh hypothesis/watchlist/alert rows missing required incident ids while warning on legacy rows. |

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make Event Alpha quality verdicts cap watchlist lifecycle state | — | Final opportunity-quality verdicts now cap lifecycle state before active-watchlist, monitor, router, alert snapshot, daily brief, inbox, and research-card consumers see rows. `local_only`, insufficient-data, and zero-score rows cannot remain active `WATCHLIST`/`HIGH_PRIORITY`; they persist as `QUALITY_BLOCKED`/local review rows with requested/final state and a `quality_state_block_reason`. Valid `watchlist`/`high_priority` verdicts still allow active watchlist states, and `EVENT_PASSED`/`ARMED` lifecycle progression remains possible for validated watchlist-quality rows. Daily briefs and quality review show `Quality-Capped Watchlist Rows` separately; artifact doctor strict mode blocks fresh uncapped state conflicts and warns on legacy conflicts. Block reasons now describe missing evidence rather than positive evidence, and candidate-discovery funnel metrics distinguish raw terms from candidate-like/accepted/context-validated/promoted candidates. `TRIGGERED_FADE_RESEARCH` remains preserved only for deterministic `event_fade.py` + `proxy_fade`. |

Signal-quality fixtures expanded to 21 cases including political meme proxy, CryptoPanic tag, URL-only false positive, candidate-discovery, and Hyperliquid/HYPE identity cases. |
